76152.subject: Weekly Recap: The Week Wall Street Bought In
76152.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

This week the incumbents stopped experimenting with stablecoins and started buying the whole stack. **Stripe** bid $53 billion for **PayPal**, a deal that would put Bridge and PYUSD under one owner, while **Visa** launched a stablecoin platform on **Open USD** aimed at 200 million merchants.

The plumbing went live, too: the **DTCC** began real tokenized-securities trades and **OKX** pooled tokenized stocks into one book — as Washington pushed the **Clarity Act** toward a floor vote.

Enjoy!

———————————————————————————

##### PARTNER

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/615a54ad-e163-46db-be85-a4b50f4afd00/TIME_CircleKrakenMysten_large.png)
Caption: 

_The Defiant_ and **Alumni Ventures** are teaming up this week only to give readers early [access to high-potential startup opportunities](https://www.av.vc/funds/blockchain/the-defiant-ad?utm_medium=email&utm_source=tofu&utm_content=partner-follow-up&utm_campaign=TheDefiantJulyFSend), including some of today's most exciting Blockchain & Fintech startups co-invested alongside top VC firms like Paradigm & Pantera Capital.

**You get:**

* Curated deal flow of high-potential Blockchain & Fintech startups

* No cost

* No commitment to join

* Invest only if a company excites you

**[See Blockchain & Fintech Deals Today →](https://www.av.vc/funds/blockchain/the-defiant-ad?utm_medium=email&utm_source=tofu&utm_content=partner-follow-up&utm_campaign=TheDefiantJulyFSend)**

———————————————————————————

———————————————————————————

# Top Stories This Week

## Stripe Bids $53B for PayPal, Combining Bridge and PYUSD

**Stripe**, with Advent International, made an unsolicited [~$53 billion bid for PayPal](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/stripe-s-usd53b-paypal-bid-would-combine-bridge-and-pyusd-under-one-owner). If accepted, it would fold **Stripe**'s Bridge stablecoin rails and **PayPal**'s PYUSD token into one company — a vertically integrated stablecoin powerhouse and the clearest sign yet that payments giants treat stablecoins as core infrastructure.

## Visa Launches a Stablecoin Platform Built on Open USD

**Visa** [launched an enterprise stablecoin platform](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/visa-launches-stablecoin-platform-built-on-open-usd) that crypto head Cuy Sheffield says will reach more than 200 million merchants, initially through **Open USD**, the consortium stablecoin positioned against Circle's USDC. Merchant distribution is the moat issuers cannot buy, and Visa just wired it to a USDC rival first.

## DTCC Starts Live Tokenized-Securities Trades

**The DTCC**, the plumbing that settles most US securities trades, [began live production trades of tokenized real-world assets](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/dtcc-starts-live-tokenized-securities-trades-with-more-than-two-dozen-firms) with more than two d

76151.subject: Has China obtained the world’s most important machine?
76151.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDF9AAcAAAAABCWbAcFHcLE8PQCUx7YpuPS_6ksAvlgDexQxNcrn-RweNeU7c-LI87DnwZOFBK6w9fjB_D9L30sz4kfouSL-Mm_qDWWVf5sqxxqPnFX-I79kvg 

July 18th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist This Weekend

 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDF9AAcAAAAABCWbAcFIVq86SryqNE1wuha5lpn-wYKNIe1waTa8RX2iyJbbA_n3H3q-fv60szhM9VT28jaZYBQ52XKAqIVgq6ORnWBFYZ5IU5hTKuKfkiX_mQ 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDF9AAcAAAAABCWbAcFJqAY7iA98OqY6WKGVdctWR0Aqxecq6uOCoA6XdQTeh33NX6RjmExED19HxaebHELXbpOY18MraM8-QXri-nkhR-GaAOWsf4mamCtZyw 


 


https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDF9AAcAAAAABCWbAcFKi_bPiEnkz0NOIVx42G2w8li0IIdoAzbbVpw0lK_z-7wkgWAdZXPL5UJZh_cVJMFSVTM7w6q43Z7lue36rqLlRhq4xY8a5Obz 

Photo: REUTERS


Jeremy Page

Chief China correspondent



 There is something steampunk about the extreme-ultraviolet lithography, or EUV, machine. To a layman’s eyes, this bus-sized box of pipes, wires and cylinders looks part Heath Robinson, part “Blake’s 7”. Even the wizardry inside seems to blend the industrial and information ages: it prints semiconductors by firing lasers at molten tin. Yet this is the only contraption that makes chips sophisticated enough to power the world’s leading artificial-intelligence models. And it is only produced by one company, ASML, which is Dutch.


 So I was intrigued to
